concept: Together / With, As a Way of Doing
description: Together: the manner of doing something as more than one. Built on 'nua' (with): where 'nua' relates two nouns, 'nuawe' describes the doing itself. Like 'maeli' (quiet), it is an adjective that does its most common work in preverbal position, where Phi puts manner.
sound_symbolism: 'nua' carries the withness and 'we' softens it into manner. The word is the preposition taught to move.
grammatical_notes: Adjective, most often preverbal like 'maeli' (quiet): 'lo mia nuawe thalo.' (We walk together.) 'lo mia nuawe menui.' (We dwell together.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Nothing is done alone anyway; the word just admits it. Interdependence at the scale of a single verb.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Collaboration has a grammar, and this is its adverb: any verb, done as more than one, without merging anyone.

tags.spatial: Side by side in the verb
